subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor's temporary access privileges against data governance policies. Check if contractor role has authorization to download full database backups, whether local analysis downloads are permitted, and what compliance controls should apply.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier. This query explicitly asks for governance and access control evaluation—a core policy_action use case. Mid tier is appropriate because the evaluation must reason through multiple policy dimensions (contractor role, data sensitivity, local analysis restrictions, and compliance controls) rather than a simple single-rule check. No additional subtasks needed: the policy check itself determines authorization and generates the governance decision without needing to cross-reference historical SQL data or reconcile multiple sources.

output.reason: Contractor-dave lacks authorization to download full database backups under multiple policies. POL-701 restricts backups to full-time DBA employees only. POL-702 explicitly prohibits contractors from raw data exports, limiting them to de-identified query results. POL-703 requires explicit project manager approval and 7-day advance notice for any contractor data access, which has not been documented.
applied_constraints: Action denied: Full backup download not permitted for contractor role
Required: Project manager explicit written approval
Required: 7-day advance notice period before any data access
Alternative: Redirect to de-identified query results process
Escalation: Route request to data governance authority for conditional approval if business need justified
